How fresh is this? Full rebuild end-of-day after the close on the 2026-07-14 data, then refreshed hourly during US market hours (prices, intraday options-flow signals; the AI reads stay on the EOD batch). Stock & technical screeners update once a day (EOD), options-flow screeners (Intraday ~30 min) refresh during the session. Insider feeds are daily, 13F is quarterly. Each card shows its own refresh cadence and data date. Screeners are ordered highest data confidence first.

★ Cross-screener confluence

CONFLUENCE
🧠 Flags tickers appearing on 2 or more coherent screeners within one trading day, concentrating signal where independent models agree.
Backtested combos first (May-2026 sweeps + pairs battery), then today's coherent overlaps. Trade-ready funnel: best_trades_today →
Validated & debunked combos — numbers, not vibes

Generic confluence carries the bucket-level stats only (3+ VALID screeners: WR 81.7% at +20d). A pair NOT listed above has no proven pair-level edge - treat the overlap as attention, not size. New-screener pairs re-enter the battery as their history grows.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

CVS scores 211 on CLENOW_MOMENTUM, PULLBACK, and VOLUME_LEADER - the top name today and a clean three-way overlap with no mixed signals. MS at 183 stretches across four screeners including VARIANCE_PREMIUM and VOLUME_LEADER, the broadest coverage in the batch, and CAT at 187 adds VARIANCE_PREMIUM to the same CLENOW plus PULLBACK base. No ticker hits a validated combo today (matched_combo null across the board), which removes the highest-confidence statistical tier from the output entirely. Score_stats reports only n_overlaps=20 with no distribution breakdown, but the visible picks are top-heavy - three names between 183 and 211 then a hard drop to a cluster of three at 98 - and the NEUTRAL macro at 54.25 adds no directional tailwind. Take CVS first at moderate conviction, capped by the absence of any VALID combo match.

🎈 Blow-off top detector NEW

BLOWOFF_DETECTOR
🧠 Spots parabolic tops about to roll over on the top-sector winners — Greenwood-Shleifer-You 2-year rallies, Edwards-Magee parabolic, gap exhaustion, island reversals and LPPLS variance acceleration. Only the max-drawdown-backtested signals carry weight; the academic heuristics confirm. GO / NO GO on naked puts comes from the live Polygon options chain.

Read
Top blow-off candidate today: PNC (score 4, target no v2 estimate).

0 ticker(s) fire at least ONE Bonferroni-validated backtested signal (25y, 50,109 obs, p<0.001). 0 stack ≥3 points = high conviction.

Methodology: the backtest-validated signals measure max drawdown over 20d (not the mean forward return — masked by rebounds in a bull market). The academic heuristics (Greenwood-Shleifer-You, Edwards-Magee, gap, island, LPPLS) stay displayed for context but do not survive Bonferroni on their own — they only confirm.
How to read this
Backtest-validated signals (25 years, 91 tickers, 50,109 observations, p<0.001):
• Two-red parabolic (weight 3) — 2 red days after parabolic. DD 20d -11.55%, lift +20pp, N=302.
• Stoch cross<80 + parabolic (weight 2) — DD 20d -9.65%, N=148.
• Rally + red on volume (weight 2) — rally 10%+ then red day. DD 20d -7.23%, N=93.
• Shooting star / BB fail / Bearish engulf (weight 1) — DD 20d -3 to -5%.

Options decision = multi-factor composite 0-100 (IV Rank · IV-HV · term struct · skew 25Δ · implied/DD ratio · liquidity). Score ≥70 + liquidity OK → 🟢 GO ATM Put. Score 50-70 → 🟡 GO LIGHT OTM 25Δ. <50 or liquidity KO → 🔴 NO GO + reason.

Global composite REJECT on its own (mean forward return bull-biased). Individual signals VALID on the max-DD metric.

🧠 Detects institutional positioning by pairing unusual options sweeps with next-day OI delta confirmation and EMA 8/21 trend alignment.
Construction
Unusual options sweep + next-day OI delta confirmation (OPENING vs CLOSING) + EMA8/21 structure + low-OI anomaly. Score includes OI Delta, premium $, signal persistence, EMA alignment.
Top picks: $QQQ
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d
1QQQ ↑50 Watch$719.69 +1.12%BULLISH SWEEPCALL721$1589KNOT_FOUND—79STRONG47
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Starts from the prior day's UNUSUAL signals, then checks T+1 OI Delta
  • OI Delta: OPENING (+25), LIKELY_OPENING (+15), CLOSING (-50), NEUTRAL (+5)
  • 20d flow persistence: net≥5 → +30, ≥3 → +20, ≥2 → +10, ≤-2 → -15; streak bonus ≥3
  • Multi-leg spread detected: -30; Low-OI anomaly: +15
  • EMA STRONG +10 / PULLBACK +5 / BROKEN -10; quality HIGH +5 / LOW -5 (min mcap $500M)
  • Archives only non-CLOSING signals AND score ≥ 50
Score
clamp(0,100, OI_delta + persistence + spread + low_oi + ema + quality). CLOSING dropped. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape confirms nothing: only 5 of 290 signals are resolved, well short of the 20-trade minimum needed for a verdict, so the 100% win rate and 200% average return are noise and the monthly cohort 100% win rates across April through June offer no meaningful support for the 68.1% backtest figure.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Liquid universe: market cap ≥ $2B, volume ≥ 500K
  • VP spread (IV Rank vs HV30) - weight 35%
  • Sinclair sector bias (Utilities +31% … Tech -8%) - weight 25%
  • Value factors (P/E, RoE, RoA, D/E, mcap) - weight 25%; IV Rank - weight 15%
  • Garbage-stock bonus (high P/E, low RoE) up to +15
Score
clamp(0,100, vp×0.35 + sector×0.25 + value×0.25 + ivr×0.15 + garbage_bonus). LONG (sell puts), top 30.

🧠 The 90-day tape (78.8% WR, avg +3.7%, beats SPY -0.3% by +4.0 pts, verdict KEEP) confirms the backtest, and the monthly cohort trend reinforces it - April's 50.8% win rate trough recovered to 60.3% in May and 82.5% in June while average return held near +4.1% throughout, the key number being that recent cohorts are running above the 90-day aggregate on both metrics.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

DUK and EXC top today's batch at 77 (Signal tier), PCG follows at 76 - all utilities, the sector Sinclair's framework tilts toward with a structural +31% bias, so the setup carries theoretical backing. NEE and XEL round out the utility sweep at 75 before PEP and CPB bring defensive staples into the mix. The 38 signals cluster tightly between 65 and 77, median 70, no name clearing 80, a compressed distribution with no breakout candidate. Macro sits NEUTRAL at 54.25, which neither accelerates nor kills a variance premium approach, and utilities' low-beta character suits a directionless tape. Start with DUK at medium conviction - tied at the top score and typically carrying the deepest utility options liquidity - and hold off adding size until a name clears 80.

🧠 Finds stocks where IV tracks realized vol in the MID variance risk premium tertile, avoiding overpaid options (HIGH) and market-disbelief names (LOW).
Construction
Longs tickers in the MID tertile of the variance risk premium (IV vs realized): options priced coherently with realized vol, neither too cheap (LOW = market disbelief) nor too rich (HIGH = catalyst already priced).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads the variance_premium_daily table on the latest day
  • Keeps only the MID tertile (OOS sweet spot: neither LOW cheap nor HIGH catalyst-priced)
  • Sorted by vrp_proxy ascending, top 30
Score
max(50, 100 − rank×50/n) within the MID tertile. LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

HPQ leads at 100, EWY at 99, and WULF at 97, all rated Very Strong with no pattern context available to further differentiate them. The score distribution is top-heavy: 13 of 30 signals clear 80 and 22 of 30 clear 65, against a median of 75, meaning this is not a thin or marginal batch. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.25 carries no directional tailwind for the strategy but does not argue against it either. Preference is HPQ first at high conviction on the clean 100 score, with EWY as a close second given its broad Korean equity exposure adds diversification alongside a near-perfect read.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • For each ticker on the latest options_daily_flow day
  • Trigger: put_volume ≥ 3.0× the 30d moving average of put_volume
  • AND put_volume ≥ 100 contracts
Score
min(100, put_ratio × 20) where put_ratio = put_volume / 30d average. LONG (contrarian: hedging = bottom near).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

All 50 signals score 100 with no pattern field populated, meaning the screener is pegged at ceiling and offers zero intra-batch ranking today. Among the eight shown, ARWR and CLDX are the more interesting names because biotech put surges in stable price action carry sharper IV-crush setups than most sectors, and AEP stands out as a large-cap regulated utility where institutional hedging flows are structurally more deliberate. The score distribution is fully degenerate, every single name at 100 out of 100, which reads as a scoring model failing to discriminate rather than an unusually clean batch. Macro is NEUTRAL at 54.25, neither suppressing nor accelerating this contrarian thesis. If forced to act, AEP first at low conviction, since the put-hedge-in-calm-tape logic is most coherent for a utility, but the uniform ceiling scores make the whole batch suspect.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Excluded if pulled back > 25% below the 52-week high
  • Uptrend: 90d slope > +0.20%/day, 60d slope > +0.25%/day, 90d R² > 0.70, price > EMA50 and EMA200
  • Pullback in progress: 30d slope < +0.15%/day, 10d slope < +0.05%/day, RSI(14) between 40 and 65 (sweet spot 45-55)
  • Healthy pullback: 10d/30d volume < 0.80, ATR contracting, no down gaps, Fibonacci level 38/50/62%, <15% below 52w high
  • Recovery: higher lows, rising 5d volume, bullish RSI divergence
Score
Uptrend (max 30) + Pullback (max 25) + Healthy (max 25) + Recovery (max 30). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live tracking partially contradicts the backtest: the 90-day win rate of 52% trails the backtested 65%, but the average 20-day return of +2.2% still beats SPY by 0.8 points and the monthly cohort trend supports staying the course - the February 2026 trough (24.6% WR, -3.3% avg) has given way to three straight improving months, with June reaching 61.7% WR and +2.7% avg.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
FULL SIZE OKWR 52% · avg +2.2% · beats SPY +1.4% by +0.8 pts
Last 90 days: 620 signals captured · 491 resolved at +20d · WR 52% · avg +2.2% · vs SPY +1.4%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 498 closed · WR TB 40% · 22% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

MGM leads at a perfect 100 with the LAUNCH pattern, making it the clearest candidate in today's batch. 🧠 AI read · today's batch

Four names - IDCC, NNE, PPTA, and SMR - share the session high of 94 (Very strong), with IE and OKLO close behind at 91; pattern data is absent for all eight names, making score and tier the only available differentiators between them. The distribution is top-heavy and compressed: median 84, 22 of 28 signals above 80, and the entire batch clearing 65, meaning almost no weak filler is diluting today's basket. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (54.25), which neither aids nor undermines the trade, as this screener's 120-day horizon is structurally macro-agnostic by construction. First preference is a small-weight basket anchored by IDCC, NNE, PPTA, and SMR using LEAPS sizing per the backtest design, split equally across all four until tape confirmation favors overweighting one name.

🧠 Spots vol-of-vol exhaustion using VVIX percentile, VIX term structure, FOMC proximity, and weekend effect to flag sell-vol entries at peak fear.
Construction
VVIX percentile + VIX term structure (VIX3M/VIX) + FOMC proximity + weekend effect. Detects 'vol-of-vol' climax (sell-vol opportunities at the top of fear).
Top picks: $SPY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayVerdictComposite
1SPY ↑64 Watch$751.83 +0.36%SELL VOL1.64
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market-level signal archived under SPY (Sinclair composite)
  • VVIX: ≥90th pctile → SELL VOL (+2), ≤10th → BUY VOL (-2)
  • Term structure VIX3M/VIX < 1.0 contango → +1; VIX regime 13-19 sweet +1, >24 danger -1
  • Weekend (Friday) +0.5; FOMC 3d ahead +0.5; Hurst/timing optional
Score
sum of sub-scores (~-6 to +6). ≥3.5 STRONG SELL VOL … ≤-3.5 STRONG BUY VOL. Archived score = clamp(0,100,(total+6)/12×100).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape confirms a real but narrow edge - 69% WR and avg +1.5% beats SPY's +1.4% by just 0.1 points - and while the monthly cohorts hold a winning rate (June 71%, May 61%), the avg return has compressed sharply from April's 5.4% to June's 0.6%, with the Signal tier's 87.5% WR on 8 trades the single number that separates this screener from noise and argues for waiting on tier upgrades before sizing.

🧠 Flags stocks with options volume above 5x the 20-day mean, significant premium, and high vol/OI to surface probable institutional positioning ahead of moves.
Construction
Stocks with options volume > 5× mean(20d) + premium > $50K + Vol/OI > 5. V3 filters now applied at write time: keeps only calm (vol<1.5× avg) and non-extended (±5% from EMA20) tickers, drops LATE-timing chasing signals.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Per-contract filters: Vol/OI > 3.0×, volume > 500, premium > $100K, contract OI ≥ 10, chain OI ≥ 5000
  • Expiration 7-365 DTE; deep ITM excluded
  • Pattern: delta>0.7 → HEDGE, call → BULLISH SWEEP, put → BEARISH SWEEP
  • Timing: LATE (-15) if chasing a >3% move, EARLY (+10)
  • V3 hard filters (before archive): volume < 1.5× the 20d average, |price-EMA20| < 5%, timing ≠ LATE
Score
Vol/OI (max 30) + log premium (max 30) + OTM (max 20) + delta (max 20) + timing (-15/+10) + IV compression (IVRank<15 +15). Archived if ≥50.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape shows 100% win rate across just 16 resolved trades averaging 194.72% against SPY at 0.11%, but the explicit WATCH verdict flags insufficient sample size, leaving the backtest's own MARGINAL rating at p_bonferroni 0.097 as the more reliable anchor.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

XLI at 91 is the only Very strong name and the sole score above 80, but its BEARISH SWEEP paired with a LONG direction tag flags institutional put-buying on industrials, not a clean directional long. QQQ at 69 shows the same pattern - BEARISH SWEEP, LONG direction - reinforcing that the dominant smart flow today is macro hedging rather than upside positioning. COIN at 68 with a confirmed BULLISH SWEEP is the cleanest read in the batch and the only top-tier name where flow and direction fully align. The score distribution is thin: median 61, five names between 65-69, three sitting at 60-61, with nothing bridging the 70-80 gap. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.25 provides no tailwind and this batch skews soft overall - COIN is the first choice at moderate conviction.

Construction
Classifies 11 GICS sector ETFs into 8 categories: EARLY_ENTRY, RIDING, LATE_CHASE, EXHAUSTED, DECELERATING, NEW_DOWN, DEAD, NEUTRAL. Uses strength + acceleration + RS crossover vs SPY.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

XLE and XLC are the only names that matter today, both scoring 95 (Very strong), but with no pattern data attached the signal is a directional call, not a setup. XLF at 65 is the lone Signal-tier name, sitting too far below the leaders and not compelling on its own. The rest of the batch - four Watch names clustered at 55 and XLP at 50 - is noise, and a median of 55 with only 2 of 11 signals above 80 makes this a thin, top-heavy day, not a broad sector thrust. Macro is NEUTRAL at 54.25, which offers no tailwind for momentum acceleration plays. Take XLE first over XLC at low conviction - energy tends to hold better in neutral-regime tapes - and wait for a confirmed pattern before sizing up.

🧠 Flags stocks off 40% from highs on capitulation volume and a reversal candle, scoring drawdown depth and ATR contraction for exhaustion bounces.
Construction
Deep oversold (≥-40% from 52w high) with capitulation volume + reversal candle. Score combines drawdown depth, ATR contraction, volume spike, and proximity to 52w low. ATR-based stop 2.5×, target 3.0×, max hold 15 days.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Downtrend: 90d slope < -0.20%/day, 60d slope < -0.25%/day, accelerating decline
  • Oversold: RSI(14) < 30, price < -15% below EMA20, < -30% below EMA50, Bollinger %B < 0, <20% above 52w low
  • Capitulation: 10d/30d volume > 1.50, ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 red days out of 10, ≥2 down gaps, reversal bar
  • Signs of Strength: slope deceleration, higher lows, 1-5 day bounce off the low, bullish RSI divergence
  • Persistence bonus: signal seen ≥10 times in 15d = +20, ≥5 times = +10
Score
Downtrend (max 30) + Oversold (max 25) + Capitulation (max 45) + SOS (max 35) + Persistence (0/10/20). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape at 55% win rate and +5.3% average beats SPY by 3.9 points, which confirms the backtest direction, but the monthly cohort trend is the sharper read: January and February posted WR below 40% and deeply negative averages, while March through June recovered to 53-61% WR and positive returns, meaning this signal is regime-sensitive and the recent cohorts are the right anchor, not the full-sample 63% backtest win rate on 90 statistically marginal trades.

SOC at 100 is the only name worth leading with today, a perfect score in the Very strong tier with a confirmed breakout pattern and nothing else in the batch remotely close. FSM and VWAV both land at 80 in the Strong tier, but live tracking shows Strong-tier signals average -2.1% over 20 days at just 36% win rate, so those scores overstate their usefulness here. The distribution is middling: median 68, only 3 names above 80 out of 50 signals, with the bulk of the batch clustered just above the 65 threshold. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (54.25), giving no directional tailwind to a mean-reversion setup that already depends on timing. SOC first at high conviction, the Strong-tier pair at minimal size or pass entirely given what the live tier data says.

Data confidence HALF SIZE · Sharpe 0.73 Stocks-on-the-Move, walk-forward Sharpe 0.73 (+45.6% vs SPY); trend-quality factor, not a per-trade trigger Full methodology →
🧠 Scores stocks on annualized 90-day exponential-regression slope times R-squared, filtered by MA100 and SPY regime gate; ranks trend quality, not event triggers.
Construction
Andreas Clenow 'Stocks on the Move' (Trading Evolved ch.12). Score = annualized exponential-regression slope (90d) × R², filtered on price > MA100 and max recent gap < 15%. Buys only when SPY > MA200 (regime gate). ATR-based sizing (10 bps risk/position).

🧠 Fires a contrarian SPY long when two or more major intermarket pairs breach their 7-day correlation z-score by at least 2 sigma.
Construction
Counts intermarket correlation breaks (|z| >= 2 sigma over 7d) across major pairs from intermarket_corr_history; >= 2 broken pairs fires a contrarian BUY on SPY, severity scales with the count.

Data confidence HALF SIZE doctrine 8-step: MARGINAL at h=5d (Sharpe 0.5-0.6), REJECT at h>=10d; 60d academic drift not reproduced Full methodology →
🧠 Detects post-earnings continuation by scoring EPS surprise magnitude, gap size, post-gap hold, and analyst revision activity to identify drift candidates.
Construction
Post-earnings continuation: positive EPS surprise + gap up + holds gap into 1-3 weeks. Score uses surprise magnitude, gap size, post-gap action, and analyst revision activity.
Top picks: $BB
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySurpriseConsistencyPost-earn ret
1BB ↑95●●● Very strong$11.01 +2.80%50.0%420.7%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • POSITIVE direction (EPS beat), surprise ≥ 5.0%
  • Days since earnings in [1, 20]
  • Consistency (beats in the last 4 quarters) ≥ 2
  • Price ≥ $5, 20d avg volume ≥ 500K; final inclusion if score ≥ 45
Score
surprise (≥20 +40, ≥10 +30, ≥5 +20) + consistency (≥4 +25, ≥3 +15, ≥2 +8) + window (≤3d +20, ≤7 +15, ≤14 +10) + drift (>3% +15, >0 +10). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d tracking (56% WR, +3.6% avg, beats SPY by 3.2 pts) gives a KEEP verdict that partially offsets the MARGINAL backtest, but the June 2026 cohort at 37% WR and -0.64% avg is the contradicting signal - the recent tape has turned cold, and that number is what matters.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Stealth institutional accumulation over 60d of OHLCV (optionable universe)
  • Block day: dollar-volume z-score ≥ 1.5σ vs the 60d baseline (excluding the last 5 days)
  • Stealth: low price impact = low |return| / σ_returns
  • Persistence: number of the last 5 days with dollar-volume z > 1.0; up-day bonus
Score
clamp(vol_z/4)×40 + stealth×30 + (persist_5d/5)×20 + (10 if up_day). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d live tape (WR 53%, avg -2.4% vs SPY +0.7%, KILL verdict) contradicts the monthly cohort trend where April, May, and June each produced positive average returns, and the key number resolving that contradiction is the Strong tier's -11.8% average on 33 live trades, which drags the aggregate into negative territory and directly undermines six of today's eight picks.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

PEPG at 96 and CWAN at 93 are the only Very strong names in today's batch, both LONG with no pattern detail attached, meaning the score rests entirely on premium size, vol/OI ratio, and OI confirmation. UGP at 89 and DUBS at 88 lead the Strong tier, but the 90d live data shows Strong-tier trades averaging -11.8% on 33 resolved trades, so the label does not mean what it sounds like. Score distribution is broad and elevated, median 76, 18 of 50 signals at or above 80, all 50 clearing 65, yet that breadth matters less than tier composition given how badly Strong has performed live. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.25 adds no directional tailwind. PEPG is the first name, low conviction, sized small, with CWAN as the only backup worth considering, and the six Strong-tier names should sit unless you have a fundamental catalyst independent of this screen.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Polygon universe: ≥ 260d history, avg dollar-volume ≥ $200K, price ≥ $1
  • Entry: daily close crosses above the daily EMA(200)
  • Mandatory weekly filter: weekly close > weekly EMA(50)
  • Window: cross within the last 5 trading days
Score
60 + freshness×30 (= (5−bars since cross)/5) + 10 if weekly filter active, capped at 100. LONG.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

All 50 signals in today's batch score 100 with no pattern data attached, leaving the distribution ceiling-compressed and impossible to rank internally. BA is the only name in the visible slice with meaningful options liquidity and institutional flow; AVGG, AVGX, BCD, BATT, and the remaining vehicles are thin enough that the cross itself carries less weight. The critical wrinkle: the 'Very strong' tier covers all of today's signals, yet that cohort is the weakest in 90-day live tracking at 58% WR and +2.4% avg, well behind 'Strong' at 73.6% WR and 'Signal' at 79.3% - today's batch is paradoxically the historically softest segment. The NEUTRAL macro regime (54.25) adds no directional conviction. Preference is BA at low conviction, entered on the first intraday pullback from the cross rather than at the open.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 300K, market cap $200M-$50B
  • Mandatory gate: short interest ≥ 10% of float AND days-to-cover ≥ 3.0
  • Short interest: ≥30% float (+40), ≥20% (+30), ≥15% (+20)
  • Days to cover: ≥8d (+25), ≥5d (+15), ≥3d (+8)
  • Catalyst (not a falling knife): price > SMA50 AND 20d return > +5% (+20)
  • Volume building 10d/30d > 1.3 (+10), RSI(14) 40-70 not extended (+5)
Score
sum of components 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live record (WR 62%, avg +6.0%, beating SPY by +4.0 pts) supports KEEP, and the monthly cohort trend from April (-0.7% avg) through May (+4.0%) to June (+5.2%) confirms the tape is improving, but the decisive number is the tier inversion - Very strong names average -3.3% while Signal names average +8.1%, meaning the screener's top scores are a contraindicator.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ANAB at 95 and BV at 90 carry the two highest scores but both sit in the Very strong tier, which is the worst-performing cohort in live tracking at a 31% win rate and -3.3% average return, so their headline scores are a red flag, not a green light. ABEO, ABX, and ACHC at 85 in the Strong tier are the more credible candidates, backed by a tier that runs 57% win rate and +3.7% average over 90 days. The score distribution is artificially compressed: all 50 signals clear 65 and only 12 exceed 80, so the batch clusters near mid-range with thin separation between names. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (54.25), which is a soft backdrop for squeeze setups that depend on momentum and risk appetite to ignite. First preference is ABEO or ABX, Strong tier, low conviction given the macro drag.

🧠 Ranks Russell 1000 stocks by relative volume across three lookback windows times multi-horizon price momentum, surfacing names where volume surge and price trend confirm each other.
Construction
Reproduces layer #5 of the @kerberos007 thread ('Volume leader screen from RUT-1000'): stocks ranked by relative volume (V/10d, V/30d, V/90d avg) × multi-horizon price momentum (1d/5d/20d/90d). Filters 20d momentum > 0 and price > 5. Score 0-100 = percentile rank of the leader score across the eligible universe.

🧠 Buys the first weekly breakout above prior-week high after a red-candle pullback within four weeks of a positive earnings gap-up.
Construction
Weekly red-candle pullback within first 4 weeks post-earnings gap, then weekly close above prior week's high. Identifies high-quality continuation entries on positive earnings reactions.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked

AEHR at 100 and ARBE at 97 lead today's batch as Very strong, but patterns are null for every candidate so setup quality beyond the score is opaque. AIRO and ANGO both score 87 in the Strong tier, and live tracking shows Strong has outperformed Very strong over 90 days (avg +0.67% vs -3.35%), making that tier the lesser-bad option within a bad screener. All four names score at or above 80 with a median of 87, meaning the batch is tight and top-heavy with no differentiation at the margin. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.25 adds no tailwind and no reason to be aggressive. If forced to act, AIRO or ANGO in the Strong tier is the first look at low conviction only, with size cut well below normal given the screener's current KILL status.

Data confidence STARTER backtest_dalton_excess 2026-04 (500 tickers 2022-2026): raw excess = coin flip (WR 51%); WITH extreme-location + tail>=1.8xATR filter +3.18% @10d, N=74 only, no block bootstrap Full methodology →
🧠 Flags daily rejection tails at least 1.8x ATR at Dalton value-area extremes, where backtested excess reversals turn positive.
Construction
Market Profile on daily bars (Dalton, Mind Over Markets): rejection-tail bars >= 1.8x ATR formed at value-area extremes (location <= 30% or >= 70%), the only configuration that backtested positive.
Top picks: $RPHS
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySideTail ATRLoc %Kerberos trap
1RPHS ↑90●●● Very strong$8.79 -0.28%LOW1.8126.8%·
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Excess bar detected (wick ≥ 1.8×ATR14) formed in the last 3 bars
  • LONG direction: excess on the LOW side AND trade location ≤ 30%
  • Universe: last_close > 0, max 500 tickers
Score
tail_atr × 50 (tail_atr = wick / ATR14). LONG.

RPHS is the only name today, scoring 90 and landing in the Very Strong tier as a long setup, but pattern is null so the specific tail structure is unconfirmed by the data. A one-name batch with both the median and max score at 90 is the thinnest distribution possible for this screener, signaling how few setups cleared the combined location and tail filters today. NEUTRAL macro at 54.25 adds no directional edge for a reversal trade. Take RPHS first if you size it at all, but treat this as a single observation on a setup with thin statistical backing and no corroborating names to widen the trade idea.

Exact criteria & score formula

🧠 Filters SMART_FLOW entries by excluding trades near KVO signal crosses or Force Index flips, removing volume-distorted momentum noise.
Construction
SMART_FLOW signals filtered by skipping any trade with KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS or FORCE_INDEX_FLIP active ±3 days. Volume rules act as a negative filter (combo OR-skip).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads SMART_FLOW signals (2-day lookback)
  • Negative filter: SKIP if KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS OR FORCE_INDEX_FLIP volume rule active ±3 trading days
  • T+1 OI Delta filter: SKIP if call_volume(T+1)/call_volume(T) < 0.50
  • Keeps only signals that pass both filters
Score
original SMART_FLOW score, unchanged (no re-scoring). LONG.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • FOMO momentum required: 5d perf ≥ +3.0% AND 4-week perf ≥ +8.0%
  • Confirmed breakout: close > 2% above resistance (60d high) + day volume > 1.5× the 20d average (CONFIRMED +20, else WEAK +5)
  • Regime gate: not archived if VIX > 20 (OOS decay -0.49 Sharpe at VIX 20-25)
Score
perf_5d×0.3 + perf_4w×0.3 + perf_13w×0.2 + return_1y×0.2 + breakout_points (+20/+5). Not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled (top pick = 100). LONG.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Parabolic trend: 90d slope > +0.25%/day, acceleration (30d > 60d > 90d)
  • Over-extension: RSI(14) > 70, price > +15% above EMA20, > +30% above EMA50, Bollinger %B > 1.0
  • Exhaustion: volume divergence (price up, volume down), ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 green days out of 10, ≥2 up gaps
  • Signs of Weakness: 1-5 day rollover off the high, bearish RSI divergence
Score
Trend (max 30) + Extension (max 25) + Exhaustion (max 40) + SOW (max 35). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. SHORT.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms the backtest REJECT: 45% win rate and avg -1.3% against SPY +1.46% is a 2.8-point drag, and the monthly cohort trend deteriorates from the March 2026 artefact (67.98% WR, +9.08% avg) straight down to June 2026 at 44.98% WR and -1.71% avg on 3,204 signals, the key number being that the largest and most recent cohort is also the worst-performing.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

CRNX and NRIX are the only two Strong-tier names today, both scoring 80 with a TRIGGER pattern, reflecting maximum vertical slope, extended MA20 distance, and elevated call premiums - the sharpest technical setup this screener can produce. BLZE and DFTX follow at 75 in SOW ZONE, with ATEX, DRTS, EXFY, and RNAC rounding out the batch at 70. The score distribution is thin at the top: 50 signals, median 60, only 2 names hitting 80 and 18 clearing 65, so the batch is concentrated rather than broad, and not unusually strong. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.25 provides no tailwind for a short hedge, and with the backtest a hard REJECT and the 90-day verdict at KILL, this is a watch list at best, not a trade list. If forced to name one, CRNX at 80 is first in line, but conviction is low and sizing should reflect that.

Data confidence STARTER · edge unproven validity test Sharpe 0.98 N=8 CI extreme, doctrine-rejected solo Full methodology →
🧠 Flags stocks with weekly RSI below 30 then confirms entry via daily RSI crossover and candlestick reversal patterns, hunting mean-reversion bounces from extreme oversold readings.
Construction
Contrarian RSI(14) reversal: HARD GATE weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (coded 2026-06-09), then daily RSI oversold/crossover + candlestick reversal + demethod zone scoring.
Top picks: $CAPR $AEM $OR
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayRSI (D)RSI (W)CrossoverPattern

2AEM ↑65● Signal$144.41 +0.63%34.5025.65YESNONE
3OR ↑60 Watch$28.90 +0.59%30.3328.56YESHAMMER
4RYAM ↑60 Watch$7.23 +1.83%30.6927.67YESNONE
5DQ ↑58 Watch$11.84 +1.81%30.0617.95YESNONE
6JKS ↑58 Watch$15.55 +1.97%33.2722.37YESNONE
7AMR ↑55 Watch$153.94 +3.52%31.9623.96YESNONE
8NAUT ↑53 Watch$1.75 -1.69%27.5022.74NOHAMMER
9SSTK ↑53 Watch$7.61 -7.76%6.2024.38NOHAMMER
10ELME ↑52 Watch$1.54 +1.32%27.7825.23NODRAGONFLY
11HON ↑50 Watch$222.68 +0.19%7.9022.09NONONE
12TDS ↑50 Watch$34.26 +2.61%17.4218.84NONONE
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • HARD GATE: weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (fail-closed, added 2026-06-09)
  • RSI oversold: RSI(14) ≤ 30 (+15), ≤ 20 extreme (+10), ≥4 days under 30 (+5)
  • Reversal candlestick (hammer/doji/piercing/engulfing) on T-0 to T-2
  • RSI crossover: RSI back above 30 today or yesterday
  • Demethod zone: swing-low cluster (3% tol) + proximity to EMA20 (<3% +5) or EMA50 (+3)
  • Volume spike ≥2.0× (+5) + VIX %B regime (BTFD_BOOST +15 to DANGER -10)
Score
RSI Oversold (max 30) + Candlestick (max 25) + Crossover (max 20) + Demethod+EMA (max 15) + Volume (max 10) + Regime (-10 to +15), bounded 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 With the backtest doctrine-rejected at N=8 and all stats null, the 90-day live tape is the only evidence base, and at 54.6% WR with a +4.6% average beating SPY by 3.8 points across 496 resolved trades the aggregate looks valid, but the monthly cohort trend cuts hard against it: WR has compressed from 78.8% in March to 52.9% in June, and the May cohort printed a negative average return of -0.4%, a deterioration the headline 90-day number obscures.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is weak: 12 signals with a max of only 70, a median of 55, and zero names at 80 or above. CAPR at 70 (Signal, SETUP) leads outright and is the only name worth first-look attention given the clear gap to everything below it. AEM at 65 (Signal, SETUP) is the secondary pick, also carrying a SETUP pattern rather than the lower-conviction WATCHLIST label that fills out the rest of the list. The score distribution is compressed in the mid-range, which in a contrarian bottom screener means the hard oversold gate triggered but confirmation signals are thin, not stacked. A NEUTRAL macro at 54.25 does not block the trade, so CAPR is the preferred first entry at moderate conviction only.

🧠 Detects unusual volume, premium, and open interest shifts in crypto-linked ETFs, linking flow intensity to spot crypto momentum as a directional signal.
Construction
Tracks unusual flow on crypto-linked ETFs (BITO, IBIT, FBTC, ETHE, GBTC, etc.) - volume vs mean, premium %, OI delta. Score weights flow intensity + spot crypto correlation.
Top picks: $COIN $FBTC
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayRSIRS IBIT 20dRegime
1COIN ↑55 Watch$161.50 +2.62%44.840.4%risk_on
2FBTC ↑50 Watch$56.22 +3.86%44.030.2%risk_on
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Fixed 12-ticker universe: spot ETFs (IBIT/FBTC/ETHE/BITO) + equity proxies (MSTR/COIN/MARA/RIOT/…)
  • Filters: price > $2, 21d median USD ADV > $5M
  • Uptrend gate: SMA50 > SMA200 AND price > SMA50
  • Pullback: price between -8% and +1% vs SMA20; RSI(14) 35-55; RS vs IBIT 20d > 0
Score
Uptrend (max 35) + Pullback (max 25) + RSI (max 20) + RS (max 10) + Volume (max 10) ± regime, bounded 0-100. Archived if ≥35. LONG.

🧠 EMERGENCE_THEMES surfaces tickers held inside qualifying theme ETFs and tags each by maturity stage from EMERGING to CROWDED, betting that early-stage theme adoption precedes price discovery.
Construction
Detects emerging investment themes (e.g. Robotics, Space, AI Agents, Quantum). Tickers are extracted via ETF holdings of qualifying theme ETFs. Signal status: EMERGING / BUILDING / ESTABLISHED / CROWDED.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

IDNA, VOX, and HACK share the day's ceiling at 70, a modest high with no name clearing 80 and no pattern detail available on any pick. Today's entire visible batch is ETFs rather than single stocks, which limits the alpha surface and suggests the screener is surfacing theme wrappers rather than individual names. The score distribution is compressed - 12 of 50 signals reach 65 or above and the median sits at 60, a flat, average-heavy read rather than a top-heavy one. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.25 provides no sector tailwind for theme momentum plays. HACK at 70 gets first attention given cybersecurity spending is less cyclically sensitive than genomics or cloud, but conviction stays low until pattern signals populate and the regime lifts above neutral.

FULL SIZE OKWR 64% · avg +7.5% · beats SPY -0.9% by +8.4 pts

Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 136 closed · WR TB 35% · 35% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

All 8 top picks print a perfect 100 score in the Very strong tier, with no pattern metadata attached to any of them, which limits conviction on individual setup quality. SRTY stands out as a structural oddity - it is a 3x inverse Russell 2000 ETF scoring as a max-score LONG, embedding a small-cap short thesis inside what is formally a long signal. HDV at 100 adds a second defensive layer alongside SRTY, and in a NEUTRAL macro (score 54.25) that drift toward risk-off instruments is the most coherent read of today's batch. The score distribution is top-heavy: 8 of 50 signals sit at 100 and 41 of 50 clear 65, with a median of 70, so the batch clusters at the extremes rather than spreading smoothly. First preference is SRTY given the macro fit, at moderate conviction only, since no pattern data exists and the backtest is unvalidated.

🧠 Aggregates quarterly 13F filings from 20 hedge funds, scoring stocks on new and increased positions to surface concentrated institutional conviction.
Construction
Quarterly SEC 13F-HR filings from 20 top hedge funds via EDGAR. Whale Score: +20/NEW (cap 60), +10/INCREASED (cap 30), -5/CLOSED, -3/DECREASED. Stale 60-90 days by nature (quarterly).

🧠 AI read · today's batch

All 50 signals score exactly 100, median 100, with every name clearing both the 80 and 65 thresholds - a pure ceiling cluster that strips the screener of any internal ranking this quarter. META, ASML, and SPOT sit at the head of the output list, each tagged Very Strong and LONG, but pattern data is null across every pick so the score alone provides no basis to prefer one over another. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.25 neither blocks nor accelerates a 13F momentum play, it simply adds no directional conviction. The batch reads as broad institutional accumulation, not a precise ranked signal. First preference is META on liquidity and institutional depth, but conviction is low-to-moderate because the screener itself offers no differentiation within this batch.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

FINS tops today at 100, with SVRE and COE both at 99, all three rated Very Strong, though no pattern detail is attached to any pick. JEF at 98 is the most liquid and recognizable name in the batch, a broker-dealer where C-suite open-market buying carries information weight. The score distribution is almost meaninglessly compressed: all 40 signals clear 80, median sits at 95, which means the rank-by-value construction is doing the sorting and the bottom of the batch is barely distinguishable from the top. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.25 is not a headwind, but insider signals punch harder when the tape has direction. Take FINS first on the highest raw score, medium conviction only until pattern context is available.

Data confidence Starter · untested · no rigorous backtest yet, queued for the untested-screeners battery. Methodology →
🧠 Identifies the cheapest stocks by EV/EBIT, betting that deeply depressed operating multiples mean-revert as earnings recover or capital rotates in.
Construction
Tobias Carlisle's Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT). Universe is market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services and Utilities (EV distorted), drops negative operating earnings. Ranks the universe by multiple ascending and surfaces the cheapest names (score 100 = cheapest of the day).
Top picks: $BZ $WRD $KROS

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (EV distorted)
  • Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT, operating income)
  • Operating earnings from Polygon financials (operating_income / EBIT); EV from yfinance else proxy market cap + non-current liabilities
  • Drops negative operating earnings; ranks the universe by multiple ascending (cheapest first)
Score
score = inverse rank scaled 0-100 (cheapest of the day = 100). LONG (deep value selection).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 With no backtest to compare against, the five resolved live picks (20% win rate, -0.06% average vs SPY at +1.36%) and the 50% trailing-buy win rate on 34 observations offer the only data points, and neither confirms nor denies an edge at these sample sizes - the key number is n=5 resolved, which settles nothing.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
HALF SIZEinsufficient data (5 resolved, need ≥20)
Last 90 days: 69 signals captured · 5 resolved at +20d · WR 20% · avg -0.1% · vs SPY +1.4%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 34 closed · WR TB 50% · 6% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

BZ at 100 and WRD at 97, both Very strong tier, are the purest expressions of the screener today, though neither carries a pattern flag so the thesis is raw multiple compression with no catalyst overlay. KROS at 93 is worth flagging as an outlier since biotech operating earnings are structurally lumpy and can produce misleading EV/EBIT reads. The score distribution is top-heavy: 7 of 16 signals sit at or above 80 against a median of 73, meaning the cheapest cohort is genuinely clustered rather than scattered across the universe. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.25 is no headwind but provides no value-rotation tailwind either. With no backtest yet and live tracking far too thin to trust, treat the batch as a watch list, not actionable longs - if one name must be prioritized, BZ at 100 is the clearest read on the mechanism, at low conviction.

🧠 Detects coordinated insider accumulation via SEC Form 4, weighting cluster size, C-suite rank, and dollar value to surface informed-money entry.
Construction
SEC Form 4 cluster buys: 3+ insiders buying within 30 days, with C-suite or director rank weighted higher. Score uses cluster size, total $ value, insider rank, and recency.

13PRQR ↑50 Watch$1.70 +0.00%3✓·$244K
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • At least 1 insider buy on the ticker
  • Cluster if ≥ 3 unique insiders within a 14-day window
  • C-level detected if title is CEO/CFO/COO/CTO/President/Chairman
  • Average transaction-size tiers: ≥$200K or ≥$100K
Score
buy base (≥2 +15, 1 +5) + cluster +25 + C-level +20 + avg (≥200K +15, ≥100K +8), cap 60. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape - 63% win rate, avg +5.6% at 20 days against SPY's -0.4% - confirms the KEEP verdict, and the June and May cohorts (60-64% WR) hold that line, with April's elevated avg (+18.9%) explained by a single outlier at 539% rather than any broad cohort strength.

🧠 Within already-running sectors, surfaces individual stocks still lagging by scoring sector relative strength against stock underperformance.
Construction
Within LATE_CHASE / RIDING sectors, identifies the lagging individual stocks that haven't yet caught the sector move. Score = sector relative strength × stock under-performance.

🧠 Identifies top relative-strength tickers inside bullish sectors, scoring by 20-day RS vs SPY, sector breadth, and cluster rank to capture intra-sector momentum persistence.
Construction
Top relative-strength tickers within bullish sectors. Score = relative strength vs SPY (20d) + sector breadth + cluster ranking.

#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectorRet 1mRet 3m
1ABX ↑80●● Strong$11.14 +1.27%Financials18.7%30.6%

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M
  • Step 1: bullish sectors = sector ETF (XLK/XLV/…) with 20d return > 0
  • Step 2: score the stocks in each bullish sector, top 10/sector
Score
Momentum 0-100 = 1M Ret (30%) + 3M Ret (30%) + RS vs SPY 3M (20%) + Volume Trend (20%), normalized components. ≥70 strong. LONG.

UAL is the standout at 85, the only Strong-tier name and the sole signal above 80, with a BEAR directional read on an airline carrying meaningful exposure to fuel cost and demand softness. Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects bull traps where price breaks above the Value Area High then closes back inside, signaling trapped longs and a short entry.
Construction
Fake breakout above VAH followed by a close back inside the value area = bull trap, longs trapped, SHORT signal.
Top picks: $ALC $ATEC $AJG
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POC
1ALC ↓75● Signal$66.86 -3.10%BULL_TRAP68.3666.56
2ATEC ↓70● Signal$8.97 -7.91%BULL_TRAP9.707.46
3AJG ↓65● Signal$253.83 -2.83%BULL_TRAP261.89213.89
4ABOS ↓60 Watch$2.53 -4.17%BULL_TRAP2.792.29
5ASAN ↓55 Watch$7.46 -2.99%BULL_TRAP7.727.50
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BULL_TRAP (SHORT): close[N-1] > VAH then close[N] < VAH (failed bullish breakout rejected)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. SHORT.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 With no completed backtest to validate against (N=0 resolved trades as of the June 2026 battery), the 90-day live tape is the only reference and it returns a KILL verdict at 57.5% win rate and a 0.2-point margin over SPY, while the monthly cohort reversal from 33.3% in May to 64.5% in June makes trend direction ambiguous rather than confirming any edge.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=17 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags stocks with concurrent retail call-volume spikes, Reddit mention surges, and parabolic price moves absent an earnings catalyst, then shorts the mean-reversion.
Construction
Detects retail-driven euphoria: retail call volume spike, Reddit mention surge, parabolic price, no earnings catalyst. Score combines social attention z-score + call/put + price stretch.
Top picks: $CC $CB $BKD
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTierCall vol/OIChg
1CC ↓100●●● Very strong$18.63 +2.81%FOMO10.122.8%
2CB ↓99●●● Very strong$346.22 -2.40%EUPHORIA0.99-2.4%
3BKD ↓98●●● Very strong$14.19 -1.46%EUPHORIA0.97-1.5%
4C ↓75● Signal$133.27 -5.29%EUPHORIA0.75-5.3%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • call_volume / call_open_interest ratio (euphoria contrarian)
  • Tiers: ≥1.00 FOMO, ≥0.75 EUPHORIA, ≥0.50 GREED (scan threshold 0.50)
  • Filters: price > $10, options volume > 1000, total OI > 4000
Score
clamp(0,100, call_vol_oi_ratio × 100). SHORT (fade the retail crowd).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms the KILL verdict, with a 55% win rate generating just -0.1 points of alpha over SPY (-0.44% avg vs. -0.30% baseline), and the tiered-book win rate of 31.8% reveals the options leg is the primary drag, with June's cohort at 46.7% win rate and -2.72% avg marking a sharp step down from May's fleeting +1.79%.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=1 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Screens pre-earnings names where straddle IV underprices the Yang-Zhang HV20 expected move, betting realized volatility exceeds implied.
Construction
Pre-earnings (3-14d to event) with straddle IV < expected move from Yang-Zhang HV20. Identifies when implied vol underprices realized expectation.
Top picks: $UPS $INTC $NEE
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectionDTEExpectedPlay
1UPS ↑65● Signal$113.67 +0.69%PRE_IV_RAMP14—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
2INTC ↑55 Watch$107.76 +4.50%PRE_IV_RAMP9—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
3NEE ↑55 Watch$89.54 +1.31%PRE_IV_RAMP10—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market cap ≥ $1B
  • PRE_IV_RAMP: earnings DTE 7-14d AND IV Rank < 50 (premium not yet expensive)
  • POST_PEAD: days_since earnings 0-5d
  • REVERSAL: earnings ≤ 2d AND pre-earnings drift ≥ 3%
Score
by section: PRE_IV_RAMP (DTE + IV rank + consistency + mcap, max 100), POST_PEAD (surprise + consistency + days + drift, max 100), REVERSAL (|drift| + DTE, max 60).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

UPS is the lone Signal-tier name at 65, the only score clearing that threshold in a batch of three, with no pattern flag to reinforce the setup. INTC and NEE both sit at 55, Watch tier, pattern data absent on both. The distribution is bottom-heavy - median 55, zero names at 80 or above, one name at 65 or above - making this a thin, weak batch. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.25 provides no directional tailwind, which is a poor fit for a screener that needs catalyst-driven realized vol to beat depressed implied. UPS is the only name worth a look, at low conviction only.

EOD
Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=10 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects 25-delta put/call skew spikes versus the 90-day baseline as a contrarian long signal when the underlying stock price remains calm.
Construction
25-delta skew (put IV − call IV) spike vs 90d baseline. Bearish skew = hedging demand spike, contrarian read when stock is calm.
Top picks: $MSTR $SBUX $TSLA
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankSkew 25dPlaybook
1MSTR ↑61 Watch$97.58 +5.95%66%990.03BUY PREMIUM (IV cheap)
2SBUX ↓55 Watch$106.17 -1.09%41%770.09SELL PUT (put skew rich)
3TSLA ↑53 Watch$396.18 +0.36%38%45—BUY PREMIUM (IV cheap)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Cross-section scan of the watchlist (large caps + ETFs)
  • Skew_Z = (skew_25d − basket median) / basket std
  • IV_Rich_Z = (IV_HV_spread − basket median) / basket std
  • Composite = IV_Rich_Z×1.0 + Skew_Z×0.5; archived if |Composite| ≥ 1.5
Score
int(|Composite| × 20). SHORT vol if Composite>0 (IV rich), LONG vol if <0.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape (WR 56%, avg +7.0%, beating SPY by 5.7 points) nominally confirms the screener's value, but the key number is the tier inversion - Very Strong names produced WR 30% and avg -4.1% while Watch names produced WR 68.75% and avg +13.4%, meaning the scoring is anti-predictive at the top end, and June's cohort avg of -2.4% against a still-respectable 58% win rate suggests the return edge has compressed sharply in recent weeks.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=3 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags stocks where put IV exceeds the 90th skew percentile, detecting unusual put demand as a potential bearish catalyst signal.
Construction
Bearish IV skew bias: put IV >> call IV at extreme percentiles (>90th). Detects unusual put demand.
Top picks: $AVGO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySkew ptsIV30IV rankVerdict
1AVGO ↑100●●● Very strong$389.11 +1.32%16.7468.7%97BEAR_SKEW_EXTREME
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • 25Δ skew (30 DTE target) > +5 vol points (puts overpriced)
  • Earnings in 0-7 days (catalyst near)
  • IV Rank > 60 (high premium = fear)
  • Verdict: skew>10pts + IVRank>80 EXTREME, skew>7pts HIGH, else MILD
Score
clamp(50,100, 50 + skew_25d_pts × 3). LONG (BTFD contrarian: fear overpriced).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Nine resolved trades show 55.6% win rate but a mean 20-day return of -5.7% against SPY at +0.77%, the gap explained almost entirely by the Strong tier going 0-for-2 at -55.9% average, while monthly cohorts swing from -24.5% in June to +14.6% in May - the live record neither confirms nor refutes anything because there is no backtest to compare it against, and the WATCH verdict at N equals 9 is the only defensible call.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=25 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags stocks where 30-day implied vol exceeds 20-day realized vol by more than 1.5 sigma, targeting elevated fear premiums as premium-selling candidates.
Construction
IV30 − HV20 spread spike on individual tickers (z-score > 1.5σ on 90d). Detects unusual fear premium - candidates for premium selling.
Top picks: $MA
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30HV30RatioZone
1MA ↓85●● Strong$538.02 +0.06%54%25%2.13EXTREME_FEAR
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • ATM IV30 / realized HV30 ratio (price ≥ $10, ≥35 days of closes)
  • Zones: ≥2.0 EXTREME_FEAR, ≥1.5 FEAR, ≥1.2 ELEVATED, 1.0-1.2 NORMAL, <1.0 COMPLACENT
  • Archives only FEAR and EXTREME_FEAR
Score
min(100, ratio × 40) where ratio = IV30/HV30. NEUTRAL.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The backtest has not yet reached the required 30 resolved trades, so the monthly cohorts carry all the weight: a clear three-month deterioration from April WR 55.9% avg +10.8%, to May WR 41.9% avg -0.2%, to June WR 33.3% avg -3.6%, with the 90-day aggregate avg of +1.085% essentially indistinguishable from SPY at +1.097%, confirming the WATCH verdict and zero measurable alpha.

FULL SIZE OK
WR 68%
Refresh
EOD
Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags a close outside the value area followed by a next-session reclaim, trapping opposing positions and fueling a mean-reversion reversal.
Construction
Kerberos Apr-2020 thesis adapted to daily bars: a close below VAL (fake breakdown) followed by a reclaim next day = bear trap, shorts trapped, LONG signal. Mirror logic above VAH for bull traps.
Top picks: $ARR
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POCATR
1ARR ↑70● Signal$17.11 +1.42%BEAR_TRAP16.8917.220.31
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BEAR_TRAP (LONG): close[N-1] < VAL then close[N] > VAL (failed bearish breakout reclaimed)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape, 68% win rate and +5.30% average versus SPY's near-zero 0.045%, confirms the screener carries real edge with no formal backtest yet to anchor it, but June's cohort at 65% and +4.49% trailing May's 77% and +7.97% is the key number showing mild deceleration in the recent window.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=2 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects volatility mispricing via IV rank, IV30/HV20 ratio, skew, term structure, and institutional options flow - an experimental composite with no confirmed edge.
Construction
Research-lab composite: IV rank + IV30/HV20 ratio + skew + term structure + Kerberos-style flow. Experimental scoring R1.
Top picks: $VXX
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankHV20Rule
1VXX ↓85●● Strong$21.51 -1.24%55%847%RULE_1
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Vol-decay basket (UVXY, VXX, SVXY, SDS, QID, SQQQ, FAZ, TZA)
  • Rule 1 (3 conditions required): IV30 < 100% AND IV Rank < 10 AND VIX3M/VIX > 1.0 (contango)
  • 2/3 conditions → inactive (score 55)
Score
if 3/3 active: min(100, 85 + max(0, 3−iv_rank)). NEUTRAL.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms the KILL verdict with no ambiguity - 27 resolved trades posted a 26% win rate and avg return of -9.2% versus SPY at -0.4%, an 8.8-point gap, and the largest cohort (May, n=24) replicates that result almost exactly at 25% WR and -9.1% avg, offering no sign of recovery.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
STARTER ONLYWR 26% · avg -9.2% vs SPY -0.4% (-8.8 pts)
Last 90 days: 43 signals captured · 27 resolved at +20d · WR 26% · avg -9.2% · vs SPY -0.4%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 27 closed · WR TB 26% · 0% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 ALTMAN screens US equities using undocumented logic; the edge mechanism is not specified in available metadata.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (model not applicable)
  • Altman Z = 1.2×(WC/TA) + 1.4×(RE/TA) + 3.3×(EBIT/TA) + 0.6×(MktCap/TL) + 1.0×(Rev/TA)
  • Financials from Polygon (else yfinance)
  • Output filter: keeps only Z < 2.5 (grey + distress)
Score
score = the Z-Score itself. Zones: Z>2.99 SAFE, 1.81-2.99 GREY, <1.81 DISTRESS, <1.0 critical. LONG (risk filter).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: price > $10 (shortable)
  • Analyst gate: recommendationMean ≥ 3.0 (Hold or worse)
  • Fundamental gate: EVA Proxy Score ≤ 40 (low quality)
  • EVA Proxy 0-100 = 5 blocks of 20 pts: Profitability, Growth, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow, Valuation
Score
score = EVA Proxy (0-100, lower = worse). Top 50 sorted ascending (worst first). SHORT.

🧠 VSA detects institutional supply and demand imbalances by comparing price spread and close location to volume, flagging smart money absorption or distribution.

Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 200K, market cap > $50M
  • Per-bar metrics (last 5): relative volume vs SMA20, normalized spread, close position within the range
  • Bullish patterns: No Supply, Testing, Stopping Volume, Absorption, Falling Pressure, Failed Down Move
  • Bearish patterns: No Demand, Up-Thrust, Buying Climax, Effort To Rise Failed
  • 20-bar accumulation/distribution background + Relative Strength vs SPY 20d
Score
best-pattern priority (No Supply 3 → Buying Climax 6) + background alignment (+2/-1) + RS vs SPY alignment (+1). Top 40. LONG or SHORT depending on pattern.

Not included on this page (by design)
These Google-Sheet tabs are not per-ticker screeners, so they have no card here: ETF_RANKING (asset-class ranking), CPR_RANK (Camarilla pivot assist), SECTOR_STRENGTH / SECTOR_BREADTH / SECTOR_ROTATION (sector-level gauges, see the macro cockpit), SKEW_VELOCITY / VOLATILITY_DASHBOARD / VVIX market panel (market-level vol gauges, see the cockpit), STRUCTURAL_FLOWS (OpEx / quarter-end calendar), QQQ_PAIRS / PAIR_ZSCORE (pair-trading lab, REJECT OOS), NEWS_SENTIMENT (newsletter prep data), MULTI_STRATEGY / META_SCREENER (aggregators, superseded by Best Trades), plus journal / portfolio / content tabs. Macro composites (BTFD index, COT, Kerberos PCR) live on the cockpit and macro pages.
